title: ambrosia salsola var. fasciculata
intro: Ambrosia salsola var. fasciculata, also known as smallflower bur marigold or fascicled greasewood, is a perennial shrub in the Asteraceae family that is native to the southwestern United States and northwestern Mexico. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds that can be used to treat wounds, skin conditions, and infections. The health benefits of Ambrosia salsola var. fasciculata include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and protect against infection, making it a valuable herb for topical applications. Ambrosia salsola var. fasciculata can be prepared as a salve, cream, or tincture for use in herbal remedies, and its roots can be used as a tea to further enhance its medicinal properties.
